texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: DuPont™ Specialty Membrane XUS180804 and XUS180802 Reverse Osmosis Elements
texts.text: DuPont™ Specialty Membrane XUS180804 and XUS180802 Reverse Osmosis Elements

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Ultra-High Pressure, High-Rejection, Reverse Osmosis Elements for Industrial Water Purification
texts.text: Ultra-High Pressure, High-Rejection, Reverse Osmosis Elements for Industrial Water Purification

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DuPont™ XUS180804 and XUS180802 Reverse Osmosis Elements are ultra-high pressure elements offering an industry wide distinct combination of features:
texts.text: The DuPont™ XUS180804 and XUS180802 Reverse Osmosis Elements are ultra-high pressure elements offering an industry wide distinct combination of features:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Upto120bar (1,740 psi), ultra-high feed pressure capability due to distinct element and membrane design
texts.text: l Upto120bar (1,740 psi), ultra-high feed pressure capability due to distinct element and membrane design

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Increasing the overall efficiency of Zero-Liquid-Discharge (ZLD) by achieving very high solute concentrations thus helping to reduce the size of downstream thermal treatment
texts.text: l Increasing the overall efficiency of Zero-Liquid-Discharge (ZLD) by achieving very high solute concentrations thus helping to reduce the size of downstream thermal treatment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Excellent for recovery of salts in process streams
texts.text: l Excellent for recovery of salts in process streams

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Robust FilmTec™ reverse osmosis (RO) membrane sheet;
texts.text: l Robust FilmTec™ reverse osmosis (RO) membrane sheet;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l 34 mil feed spacer to lessen the impact of fouling on the pressure drop across a vessel and to enhance cleaning effectiveness.
texts.text: l 34 mil feed spacer to lessen the impact of fouling on the pressure drop across a vessel and to enhance cleaning effectiveness.

texts.text: Operating and Cleaning Limits

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Clean in Place (CIP) Parameters
texts.text: Clean in Place (CIP) Parameters

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. Maximumtemperature forcontinuous operation above pH 10 is 95°F (35°C).
texts.text: Maximumtemperature forcontinuous operation above pH 10 is 95°F (35°C).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. Referto guidelines in Cleaning Guidelines (Form No. 45-D01696-en) for more information.
texts.text: Referto guidelines in Cleaning Guidelines (Form No. 45-D01696-en) for more information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c. Undercertain conditions, the presence of free chlorine and other oxidizing agents will cause premature membrane failure. Since oxidation damage is not covered under warranty, DuPont Water Solutions recommends removing residual free chlorine by pretreatment prior to membrane exposure. Please refer to Dechlorinating Feedwater (Form No. 45-D01569-en) for more information.
texts.text: Undercertain conditions, the presence of free chlorine and other oxidizing agents will cause premature membrane failure. Since oxidation damage is not covered under warranty, DuPont Water Solutions recommends removing residual free chlorine by pretreatment prior to membrane exposure. Please refer to Dechlorinating Feedwater (Form No. 45-D01569-en) for more information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d. Relation between maximumallowed feed pressure and maximumfeed temperature see below
texts.text: Relation between maximumallowed feed pressure and maximumfeed temperature see below

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Referto Cleaning Guidelines (Form No. 45-D01696-en).
texts.text: Referto Cleaning Guidelines (Form No. 45-D01696-en).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Undercertain conditions, the presence of free chlorine and other oxidizing agents will cause premature membrane failure. Since oxidation damage is not covered under warranty. FilmTec™ recommends removing residual free chlorine by pretreatment prior to membrane exposure. Please refer to Dechlorinating Feedwater (Form No. 45-D01569-en) for more information.
texts.text: Undercertain conditions, the presence of free chlorine and other oxidizing agents will cause premature membrane failure. Since oxidation damage is not covered under warranty. FilmTec™ recommends removing residual free chlorine by pretreatment prior to membrane exposure. Please refer to Dechlorinating Feedwater (Form No. 45-D01569-en) for more information.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Customer Notice
texts.text: Customer Notice

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Normally, new elements are cleaned prior to initial use. The cleaning procedure should be based on the application for which the elements are to be used. If cleaning with formulated agents is not available, an alkaline wash with a wetting agent is recommended prior to initial use. Please refer to the FilmTec™ Reverse Osmosis Membranes Technical Manual (Form No. 45-D01504-en) for more information.
texts.text: Normally, new elements are cleaned prior to initial use. The cleaning procedure should be based on the application for which the elements are to be used. If cleaning with formulated agents is not available, an alkaline wash with a wetting agent is recommended prior to initial use. Please refer to the FilmTec™ Reverse Osmosis Membranes Technical Manual (Form No. 45-D01504-en) for more information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Avoid any abrupt pressure or cross flow variations on the spiral elements during start-up, shutdown, cleaning or other sequences to prevent possible membrane damage. During startup, a gradual change from a standstill to operating state is recommended as follows:
texts.text: Avoid any abrupt pressure or cross flow variations on the spiral elements during start-up, shutdown, cleaning or other sequences to prevent possible membrane damage. During startup, a gradual change from a standstill to operating state is recommended as follows:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Feed pressure should be increased gradually over a 30-60 second time frame.
texts.text: l Feed pressure should be increased gradually over a 30-60 second time frame.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Before initiating cross-flow at high permeate flux conditions (e.g., start-up with high temperature water), the set operating pressure should be maintained for 5-10 minutes.
texts.text: l Before initiating cross-flow at high permeate flux conditions (e.g., start-up with high temperature water), the set operating pressure should be maintained for 5-10 minutes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Cross-flow velocity at set operating point should be achieved gradually over 1520 seconds.
texts.text: l Cross-flow velocity at set operating point should be achieved gradually over 1520 seconds.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Avoid permeate-side backpressure at all times.
texts.text: l Avoid permeate-side backpressure at all times.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Keep elements moist at all times after initial wetting.
texts.text: l Keep elements moist at all times after initial wetting.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l To control the spread of biological growth during system shutdowns, it is recommended that elements be immersed in a preservative solution.
texts.text: l To control the spread of biological growth during system shutdowns, it is recommended that elements be immersed in a preservative solution.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reference warranty document: DuPont™ Specialty Membrane Prorated Element Warranty.
texts.text: Reference warranty document: DuPont™ Specialty Membrane Prorated Element Warranty.

texts.label: text

texts.text: Before use or storage, review these additional resources for important information:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DuPont has a fundamental concern for all who make, distribute, and use its products, and for the environment in which we live. This concern is the basis for our product stewardship philosophy by which we assess the safety, health, and environmental information on our products and then take appropriate steps to protect employee and public health and our environment. The success of our product stewardship program rests with each and every individual involved with DuPont products-from the initial concept and research, to manufacture, use, sale, disposal, and recycle of each product.
texts.text: DuPont has a fundamental concern for all who make, distribute, and use its products, and for the environment in which we live. This concern is the basis for our product stewardship philosophy by which we assess the safety, health, and environmental information on our products and then take appropriate steps to protect employee and public health and our environment. The success of our product stewardship program rests with each and every individual involved with DuPont products-from the initial concept and research, to manufacture, use, sale, disposal, and recycle of each product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DuPont strongly encourages its customers to review both their manufacturing processes and their applications of DuPont products from the standpoint of human health and environmental quality to ensure that DuPont products are not used in ways for which they are not intended or tested. DuPont personnel are available to answer your questions and to provide reasonable technical support. DuPont product literature, including safety data sheets, should be consulted prior to use of DuPont products. Current safety data sheets are available from DuPont.
texts.text: DuPont strongly encourages its customers to review both their manufacturing processes and their applications of DuPont products from the standpoint of human health and environmental quality to ensure that DuPont products are not used in ways for which they are not intended or tested. DuPont personnel are available to answer your questions and to provide reasonable technical support. DuPont product literature, including safety data sheets, should be consulted prior to use of DuPont products. Current safety data sheets are available from DuPont.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Please be aware of the following:
texts.text: Please be aware of the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l The use of this product in and of itself does not necessarily guarantee the removal of cysts and pathogens from water. Effective cyst and pathogen reduction is dependent on the complete system design and on the operation and maintenance of the system.
texts.text: l The use of this product in and of itself does not necessarily guarantee the removal of cysts and pathogens from water. Effective cyst and pathogen reduction is dependent on the complete system design and on the operation and maintenance of the system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l Any concentrate or permeate obtained from the first hour of operation should be discarded.
texts.text: l Any concentrate or permeate obtained from the first hour of operation should be discarded.
